purpose: Complete Claude Code configuration directory generated by ultrainit for the open-webui project — a full-stack AI chat platform (FastAPI + SvelteKit). Contains 36 skills, 4 domain agents, 4 shell hooks, 7 MCP server configs, and a settings.json wiring them together. Serves as the canonical reference example of what ultrainit produces for a large, real-world codebase.

architecture.overview: Two-tier assistance model: Skills handle execution workflows (how to add a feature), Agents handle structured review (checklist-driven analysis). Hooks automate enforcement (formatting, guards, reminders). MCP servers extend Claude's tool access to infrastructure. All components are stateless markdown/JSON/shell — no runtime dependencies beyond the Claude Code harness.

subdirectories.purpose: 36 workflow skills organized by function: add-* (scaffold new things), debug-* (diagnose problems), *-reference (domain knowledge), and process skills (pre-pr-checklist, cut-release). Each skill lives in its own subdirectory as SKILL.md.

4 domain specialist agents that perform structured review with checklists. Each defines a 'What to Read First' list, analysis protocol, and output format. Agents guide human decision-making rather than executing changes.

4 shell scripts wired to Claude Code hook events. PreToolUse: guard-protected-files.sh blocks writes to credentials/migrations. PostToolUse: autoformat-python.sh and autoformat-frontend.sh run formatters after edits. Stop: i18n-reminder.sh checks for missing i18n parse runs.

architecture.data_flow: Claude edits a file → PreToolUse hook fires (guard-protected-files.sh reads tool input JSON, extracts file_path, blocks if protected) → edit proceeds → PostToolUse hook fires (autoformat-python.sh or autoformat-frontend.sh reads tool input JSON, conditionally runs ruff/prettier) → conversation ends → Stop hook fires (i18n-reminder.sh checks git diff for modified .svelte/.ts files containing $i18n.t() calls, prints reminder if found). Skills are invoked by name in conversation; they inject context and step-by-step instructions. Agents are invoked for review tasks; they return structured checklists.

key_files.purpose: Wires all hooks to Claude Code events. Defines PostToolUse matchers for Edit/Write → formatters, PreToolUse matcher for Edit → guard, and Stop → i18n reminder. The single source of truth for hook activation.
7 MCP server configurations: svelte, context7, playwright, postgres, sqlite, docker, python-tools. Extends Claude's toolset to interact with infrastructure directly during development sessions.
PreToolUse guard that blocks writes to .env* files (not .env.example), .webui_secret_key, and existing Alembic/Peewee migrations. Warns on auth/permission files. Critical safety layer.
Documents the canonical ORM+Pydantic+Repository trinity pattern used for every database entity. One file, three classes (SQLAlchemy model, Pydantic DTO, static repository), module-level singleton.

Documents Alembic migration conventions: batch_alter_table for SQLite, get_existing_tables() idempotency guard, column type conventions (PK=Text, timestamps=BigInteger), server_default for NOT NULL backfills.

FastAPI CRUD route scaffold with the 3-layer access control pattern: feature gate (has_permission), admin bypass (BYPASS_ADMIN_ACCESS_CONTROL), per-resource grant (AccessGrants). Includes N+1 prevention for list endpoints.

Documents the PersistentConfig system: env.py parse at startup → AppConfig wraps in PersistentConfig → access only via .value. Explains why dict()/str() raise TypeError intentionally and why os.environ.get() reads stale values.
Ordered pre-commit checklist: format → i18n:parse → build → test → type-check → git diff --exit-code. The authoritative sequence before any PR.
Structured reviewer for Alembic migrations with a 7-point checklist. Critical because broken migrations are irreversible on production SQLite databases.

Documents the deferred-throw API client pattern used throughout the frontend: fetch → .catch stores error → if (error) throw error. Also documents the FormData upload anti-pattern (never set Content-Type manually).

Documents the non-obvious frontend chat history structure: it's a DAG (messages as a Record keyed by id, with parentId/childrenIds), not a flat array. Explains buildMessages() traversal and required Svelte reactivity triggers.
Documents the 4 plugin types (Pipe, Filter, Action, Manifold), Valves as the only persistent config mechanism, dunder param injection/stripping, and asyncio.run() pattern for event_emitter.

patterns.name: ORM + Pydantic + Repository Trinity
patterns.description: Every database entity lives in a single file with exactly three classes: (1) SQLAlchemy ORM class (singular noun, extends Base), (2) Pydantic DTO with model_config = ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) and *Model suffix, (3) static repository class (plural noun, e.g. ThingsTable) with a module-level singleton (Things = ThingsTable()). Repository methods accept Optional[Session] = None and use get_db_context(db) for session management. Convert ORM to DTO with .model_validate(orm_obj), never deprecated .from_orm().

patterns.name: Three-Layer Access Control
patterns.description: All resource endpoints enforce three ordered checks: (1) Feature gate via has_permission(user.id, 'workspace.X', config.USER_PERMISSIONS) — workspace-level flag; (2) Admin bypass via BYPASS_ADMIN_ACCESS_CONTROL.value — defaults True so admins skip Layer 3; (3) Per-resource grant via AccessGrants.has_access(user.id, 'resource', resource_id, 'read', db=db). List endpoints must use AccessGrants.get_accessible_resource_ids() for a single batch query, never per-item queries in a loop.

patterns.name: Deferred-Throw API Client
patterns.description: All frontend API functions follow: let error = null → fetch → .then(r => { if (!r.ok) throw await r.json() }) → .catch(err => { error = err.detail; return null }) → if (error) throw error. The error is the FastAPI .detail string, already extracted. Callers catch at the call site with .catch(err => toast.error(String(err))). FormData uploads MUST NOT set Content-Type header — the browser sets the multipart boundary automatically.

patterns.name: PersistentConfig Access Pattern
patterns.description: Config values defined in env.py (os.environ.get), wrapped in AppConfig via PersistentConfig('ENV_VAR', 'db.key', default). ONLY access via .value — dict(), str(), and os.environ.get() all return wrong values (TypeError, repr, or stale startup value respectively). Admin UI changes update request.app.state.config.X which triggers __setattr__ → DB persist → Redis sync.

patterns.name: Alembic Migration Safety Pattern
patterns.description: All migrations: (1) guard CREATE TABLE with get_existing_tables() check for idempotency; (2) use batch_alter_table for ALL SQLite column changes; (3) supply server_default for any NOT NULL column added to existing table; (4) type cast timestamp changes with postgresql_using for dual-DB support; (5) always implement downgrade(). PK columns are Text() (UUID strings), timestamps are BigInteger (epoch seconds).

patterns.name: Svelte Reactivity Trigger
patterns.description: Svelte only detects changes via top-level reassignment. Mutating nested properties requires reassigning the top-level variable: $settings.theme = 'dark'; $settings = $settings (both lines required). Array mutations must use spread or filter: items = [...items, newItem] (not push), items = items.filter(...) (not splice). The message history DAG (history.messages[id].content = x; history = history) follows the same pattern.

patterns.name: Hook Shell Script Pattern
patterns.description: All hooks: #!/usr/bin/env bash + set -euo pipefail, read tool input JSON from stdin (INPUT=$(cat)), extract file_path with jq, check conditions, exit 2 to block (guard), exit 0 to allow. Format hooks additionally check that the file extension and directory match before running formatters. Guard hook uses regex matching against protected path patterns.

patterns.name: Plugin Valves-Only State Pattern
patterns.description: Plugin class-level variables lose state on restart. Only Valves (pydantic BaseModel inner class with Field(default=..., description=...)) are persisted to DB. Filter plugins must include priority: int = Field(default=0) in Valves. Dunder params (__user__, __event_emitter__, __metadata__) are injected at call time and stripped from LLM-visible specs — always type as Optional with default None.

conventions.naming: Python backend: snake_case functions/modules, PascalCase classes, UPPER_SNAKE_CASE constants. Model suffixes: *Model (DTO), *Form (request body), *Response (augmented response). Repository classes: plural noun (Users, Chats, Things). ORM table classes: singular noun. TypeScript/Svelte: PascalCase.svelte components, camelCase functions/variables, onX prefix for callback props (onClose, onSubmit, onDelete), show/is prefix for boolean state (showModal, isLoading), SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for build-time constants (WEBUI_API_BASE_URL). API functions: verb + noun pattern (getThings, createThing, updateThingById, deleteThingById).
conventions.imports: Python: stdlib → third-party → local app. Local imports always from open_webui.X. Always import log from open_webui.utils.logger (loguru), never stdlib logging or print(). Svelte: Svelte core → stores → APIs → components → icons. i18n always via getContext('i18n'), never imported directly.
conventions.error_handling: Python: Always use ERROR_MESSAGES enum from constants.py, never inline strings. Always re-raise HTTPException before bare except Exception to prevent swallowing HTTP errors. Use log.exception(e) for unexpected errors. Frontend: errors from API are already the FastAPI .detail string — cast with String(err) for toast display. Validation errors surface via Pydantic automatically through FastAPI.
other: All PKs are str(uuid.uuid4()), never integers
All timestamps are int(time.time()) as BigInteger columns — never datetime objects except the legacy config table
JSON columns use JSONField (custom type), never Column(JSON), for SQLite TEXT serialization compatibility
console.log() is stripped by Vite's esbuild in production builds — use backend loguru for persistent debug output
aiohttp 3.13.3 is pinned — upgrading breaks all backend LLM API calls
npm run build not vite build — missing pyodide:fetch step breaks in-browser Python execution
After any $i18n.t() addition to .svelte or .ts files, npm run i18n:parse must be run before commit — CI gate enforces via git diff --exit-code src/i18n/

internal: backend/open_webui/models/ — entity models following ORM+Pydantic+Repository pattern
backend/open_webui/routers/ — 28 FastAPI routers, one per domain
backend/open_webui/migrations/ — Alembic migrations (current) + internal/migrations/ (Peewee, legacy frozen)
src/lib/apis/ — frontend API client functions (deferred-throw pattern)
src/i18n/ — translation files managed by i18n:parse script

external_key.name: ruff
external_key.purpose: Python formatter and linter — run by autoformat-python.sh hook after every .py edit

external_key.name: prettier (npx)
external_key.purpose: Frontend formatter for .ts/.svelte/.js/.css/.json — run by autoformat-frontend.sh hook

external_key.name: alembic
external_key.purpose: Database migration tool for current schema evolution

external_key.name: loguru
external_key.purpose: Python logging — the only approved logger, never stdlib logging or print()

external_key.name: jq
external_key.purpose: JSON parsing in all hook shell scripts to extract tool input fields

external_key.name: context7 (MCP)
external_key.purpose: Library documentation retrieval during development sessions

external_key.name: playwright (MCP)
external_key.purpose: Browser automation for E2E testing and UI verification

external_key.name: postgres/sqlite (MCP)
external_key.purpose: Direct database inspection and query during development

gotchas.issue: Vector DB collection naming is silently wrong — querying a non-existent collection name returns None/empty with no error, not an exception. The correct pattern is 'file-{file_id}' or '{kb_id}', not 'file-kb-{uuid}'.
gotchas.solution: Always verify collection names against the VectorDB adapter's naming convention in add-vector-backend/SKILL.md. Check VECTOR_DB_CLIENT.has_collection() explicitly and handle None returns from search()/get().

gotchas.issue: BYPASS_ADMIN_ACCESS_CONTROL defaults to True — in a fresh install, ALL admin users skip Layer 3 per-resource access grants entirely. Code that checks grants for admins will never run without explicitly setting this to False.
gotchas.solution: When implementing access-controlled features, test with both admin and non-admin users, and verify BYPASS_ADMIN_ACCESS_CONTROL=false in test environments to actually exercise grant checks.

gotchas.issue: filter_allowed_access_grants() silently drops over-privileged grants without returning an error. The API returns success even when grants were stripped.
gotchas.solution: Log the before/after grant count when debugging permission issues. Don't assume returned success means all requested grants were applied.

gotchas.issue: Outlet filters are skipped for direct API requests (/v1/chat/completions where request.state.direct == True). Only UI-initiated chats run outlet filters.
gotchas.solution: Test filter plugins via the UI, not direct API calls, to exercise the outlet() path. Document this limitation in plugin code.

gotchas.issue: Arena model resolution: body['model'] may be the arena model ID, not the actual model used. The real model is in body.get('metadata', {}).get('selected_model_id').
gotchas.solution: Always resolve the actual model from metadata when processing arena requests in plugins or pipeline code.

gotchas.issue: Nginx SSE buffering causes tokens to batch in 40–50 token bursts instead of streaming smoothly in production. Neither proxy_buffering off alone nor the header alone is sufficient — both are required.
gotchas.solution: Production nginx config must have both: proxy_buffering off; and add_header X-Accel-Buffering no; on the /api/ location block.

gotchas.issue: PersistentConfig raises TypeError on dict(), returns repr on str(), and os.environ.get() reads only the startup value ignoring runtime admin overrides. All three are common mistakes.
gotchas.solution: Access PersistentConfig values exclusively via .value. Never pass them to dict(), str(), or compare against os.environ.

gotchas.issue: The frontend analytics API directory is misspelled: src/lib/apis/analyics/ (missing 't'). Imports using the correct spelling will fail.
gotchas.solution: Import from '$lib/apis/analyics' (with typo) to match the actual directory name.

gotchas.issue: Two migration systems run in sequence at import time: Peewee (internal/migrations/001–018, frozen) then Alembic (migrations/versions/). Both are synchronous. Adding migrations to the Peewee system is forbidden — it is frozen at 018.
gotchas.solution: All new migrations go in the Alembic system only. Never add files to internal/migrations/.

gotchas.issue: npm run build is required (not vite build directly) because it includes a pyodide:fetch step. Skipping it produces a build that breaks in-browser Python execution silently.
gotchas.solution: Always use npm run build. The pre-pr-checklist skill enforces this.

gotchas.issue: guard-protected-files.sh blocks writes to ALL existing Alembic migration files. If you need to fix a migration that hasn't been deployed yet, the hook will block the edit.
gotchas.solution: For pre-deployment migration fixes, temporarily disable the guard hook or use --no-hook flag, then re-enable immediately after.

domain_terms.meaning: A wrapper class that syncs config values between environment variables, the database, and Redis. Values must always be accessed via .value.
The Pydantic inner class within a plugin that defines admin-configurable, DB-persisted settings. The ONLY mechanism for persistent plugin state.
Double-underscore prefixed function parameters in plugins (__user__, __event_emitter__, __metadata__) that are injected by the runtime and stripped from LLM-visible specs.
A DB record granting a specific user or group access to a specific resource at a specific permission level. Layer 3 of the access control system.
PersistentConfig flag (default True) that causes admin users to skip Layer 3 AccessGrant checks entirely.
A plugin type that acts as an LLM provider or model proxy. Its pipe() method returns the model response.
A plugin type with inlet() (pre-process request) and outlet() (post-process response) methods. Must have priority: int in Valves.
A plugin type that exposes multiple virtual models. The pipes() method returns the list of models.
A special model type used for blind A/B comparison. The actual model used is stored in metadata.selected_model_id, not in body.model.
The frontend chat history data structure — a directed acyclic graph of messages with parentId/childrenIds links supporting branching conversations, stored as Record<string, Message>.
Alembic context manager required for ALL column schema changes on SQLite, which doesn't support ALTER COLUMN natively.
Custom SQLAlchemy column type (not Column(JSON)) that serializes to TEXT for SQLite compatibility while behaving like JSON for PostgreSQL.
Context manager from internal/db.py that accepts an Optional[Session] — uses the provided session if given, otherwise creates and closes a new one. Used in all repository methods.
Feature-gate permission string checked at Layer 1 of access control (e.g., 'workspace.knowledge', 'workspace.models'). Stored in USER_PERMISSIONS config.
npm script that scans .svelte/.ts files for $i18n.t() calls and regenerates src/i18n/ translation files. Must be run after adding any new translatable string.

commands.purpose: Full frontend build including pyodide:fetch step — required, not vite build directly
Regenerate i18n translation files after adding $i18n.t() calls — must run before commit
Run prettier on all frontend files
Lint and auto-fix Python backend code
CI gate — fails if i18n files have uncommitted changes (i.e., i18n:parse wasn't run)
Generate new Alembic migration from model changes

skill_opportunities.name: chain-feature-scaffold
skill_opportunities.description: Orchestrate the full vertical slice for a new feature: config setting → entity model → migration → router endpoint → admin UI → frontend component → pre-PR checklist. Currently requires invoking 6+ skills in sequence manually.
workflow_steps: Run add-config-setting to define the feature flag
Run add-db-migration to generate and validate the Alembic migration
Run add-router-endpoint to wire CRUD endpoints with access control
Run add-admin-settings-page to expose the config in the admin UI
Run add-svelte-component to build the feature's frontend
Run pre-pr-checklist to validate the full change

skill_opportunities.name: debug-rag-full
skill_opportunities.description: End-to-end RAG pipeline diagnosis combining debug-rag-retrieval, vector backend inspection via sqlite/postgres MCP, and collection naming verification. Currently scattered across three separate skills and MCP tools.
workflow_steps: Identify which RAG stage is failing (ingestion, chunking, embedding, retrieval, reranking)
Inspect vector DB collections via MCP to verify naming conventions
Check VECTOR_DB_CLIENT.has_collection() for the suspected collection
Trace embedding model configuration via PersistentConfig
Verify web search provider if hybrid search is involved

skill_opportunities.name: review-migration
skill_opportunities.description: Automated migration safety review combining the migration-safety-reviewer agent checklist with actual migration file linting (batch_alter_table, get_existing_tables guard, column conventions).
workflow_steps: Identify the new migration file in migrations/versions/
Check for batch_alter_table on all column changes
Verify get_existing_tables() guard on CREATE TABLE
Confirm server_default on any NOT NULL columns
Run alembic upgrade head on a test database
